quizQuestion:
{___} tan alto, por favor.
(Don't speak so loudly, please.)
grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: hablar → no hables
explanation: no hables — negative tú imperative of hablar. Negative imperatives use the present subjunctive. Contrast: habla (affirmative tú, A2) vs. no hables (negative tú, B1). Pattern: -ar → no + -es; -er/-ir → no + -as.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: comer → no comas
explanation: no comas — negative tú imperative of comer. -er verb: use subjunctive form (coma → comas for tú). Affirmative: come. Negative: no comas. The switch from affirmative to subjunctive for negative is a key B1 point.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: escribir → no escribas
explanation: no escribas — negative tú imperative of escribir. -ir verb: use subjunctive form (escriba → escribas for tú). Affirmative: escribe. Negative: no escribas.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: ir → no vayas
explanation: no vayas — negative tú imperative of ir (irregular). Affirmative: ve (irregular). Negative: no vayas (uses subjunctive vaya → vayas). Complete contrast: ve al banco / no vayas al banco.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: ser → no seas
explanation: no seas — negative tú imperative of ser (irregular). Affirmative: sé. Negative: no seas (uses subjunctive sea → seas). Key irregular pair: sé / no seas.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: tener → no tengas
explanation: no tengas — negative tú imperative of tener (irregular). Affirmative: ten. Negative: no tengas (uses subjunctive tenga → tengas). Irregular pair: ten / no tengas.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: hacer → no hagas
explanation: no hagas — negative tú imperative of hacer (irregular). Affirmative: haz. Negative: no hagas (uses subjunctive haga → hagas). Irregular pair: haz / no hagas.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: decir → no digas
explanation: no digas — negative tú imperative of decir (irregular). Affirmative: di. Negative: no digas (uses subjunctive diga → digas). Irregular pair: di / no digas.

grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: venir → no vengas
explanation: no vengas — negative tú imperative of venir (irregular). Affirmative: ven. Negative: no vengas (uses subjunctive venga → vengas). Irregular pair: ven / no vengas.

quizQuestion:
{___} sin abrigo.
(Don't go out without a coat.)
sentenceTranslation: Don't go out without a coat.
grammarPointLabel: negative tú imperative: salir → no salgas
explanation: no salgas — negative tú imperative of salir (irregular). Affirmative: sal. Negative: no salgas (uses subjunctive salga → salgas). Irregular pair: sal / no salgas.

quizQuestion:
{___} tan deprisa, por favor.
(Please don't speak so fast.)
grammarPointLabel: negative usted imperative: hablar → no hable
explanation: no hable — negative usted imperative of hablar. Usted imperatives (both affirmative and negative) use the subjunctive form: hable (affirmative) / no hable (negative). Same form, different polarity.

grammarPointLabel: negative usted imperative: reflexive (no se preocupe)
explanation: no se preocupe — negative usted imperative of preocuparse. Reflexive verbs keep their pronoun before the negated form: No se preocupe (usted). Contrast: preocúpese (affirmative, attached) vs. no se preocupe (negative, before verb).

quizQuestion:
{___} todos a la vez.
(Don't all speak at once.)
grammarPointLabel: negative ustedes imperative: hablar → no hablen
explanation: no hablen — negative ustedes imperative of hablar. Ustedes imperative = subjunctive form: hablen (affirmative and negative). No hablen = don't (all of you) speak.

quizQuestion:
{___} de eso ahora.
(Let's not talk about that now.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's not talk about that now.
grammarPointLabel: negative nosotros imperative: hablar → no hablemos
explanation: no hablemos — negative nosotros imperative. Affirmative: hablemos (let's talk). Negative: no hablemos (let's not talk). The nosotros subjunctive form is used for both.

quizQuestion:
{___} más despacio, por favor.
(Please speak more slowly.)
grammarPointLabel: affirmative usted imperative: hablar → hable
explanation: hable — affirmative usted imperative of hablar. Usted imperative = present subjunctive form. -ar verb: hable (not habla, which is tú affirmative). Used in formal contexts, customer service, professional settings.

quizQuestion:
{___} a las tres.
(Come at three o'clock.)
grammarPointLabel: affirmative usted imperative: venir → venga
explanation: venga — affirmative usted imperative of venir (irregular). Uses subjunctive form: venga. Contrast with tú: ven (affirmative tú, A2) vs. venga usted. Vengan is the ustedes form.

grammarPointLabel: affirmative usted imperative: tener → tenga
explanation: tenga — affirmative usted imperative of tener. Uses subjunctive form. Common fixed phrase: ¡Tenga cuidado! (Be careful!). Contrast tú: ten (short irregular affirmative) vs. usted: tenga.

grammarPointLabel: affirmative usted imperative: decir → diga
explanation: diga — affirmative usted imperative of decir. Uses subjunctive form (diga). Also heard when answering the phone in Spanish: ¿Diga? (Hello?). Tú affirmative: di vs. usted: diga.

grammarPointLabel: affirmative usted imperative: hacer → haga
explanation: haga — affirmative usted imperative of hacer. Uses subjunctive form. Haga el favor de + infinitivo is a polite way to make requests. Tú affirmative: haz vs. usted: haga.

quizQuestion:
{___} con el profesor.
(Talk to the professor.)
grammarPointLabel: affirmative ustedes imperative: hablar → hablen
explanation: hablen — affirmative ustedes imperative of hablar. Ustedes imperative = subjunctive form for ustedes: hablen. Used throughout Latin America as the formal/plural 'you all'. Vosotros would say: hablad.

grammarPointLabel: affirmative ustedes imperative: venir → vengan
explanation: vengan — affirmative ustedes imperative of venir. Uses subjunctive form: vengan (ellos/ustedes subjunctive). Contrast: ven (tú), venga (usted), vengan (ustedes).

quizQuestion:
{___} de esto luego.
(Let's talk about this later.)
grammarPointLabel: affirmative nosotros imperative: hablar → hablemos
explanation: hablemos — nosotros imperative (let's speak). Uses present subjunctive nosotros form. Equivalent to vamos a hablar. Both are common. Negative: no hablemos (let's not talk).

grammarPointLabel: affirmative nosotros imperative: comer → comamos
explanation: comamos — nosotros imperative of comer (let's eat). -er verb subjunctive nosotros: comamos. Alternative: vamos a comer (more colloquial). The nosotros imperative is more formal/literary.

quizQuestion:
{___} al parque.
(Let's go to the park.)
grammarPointLabel: affirmative nosotros imperative: ir → vayamos (or vamos)
explanation: vayamos — nosotros imperative of ir (formal). In practice, vamos is used instead of vayamos for affirmative. Negative: no vayamos (never *no vamos for negative imperative). This is an important distinction.
